service provider.description: An organization or system that offers managed capabilities through the Platform Mesh API, managing multiple service runtimes on behalf of consumers.

service consumer.description: An organization or workload that discovers, orders, and consumes capabilities offered by service providers through the Platform Mesh account hierarchy.

control plane.description: An isolated kcp workspace providing its own set of API resources and objects, functioning as a Kubernetes-like API boundary.

account model.description: The foundational hierarchy of organizations and workspaces in Platform Mesh, powered by kcp, that governs how service providers and consumers interact.

Kubernetes Resource Model.description: The declarative, API-driven resource model from Kubernetes used as the lingua franca for service management in Platform Mesh.

capability.description: A managed service unit offered by a service provider and consumed by service consumers through the Platform Mesh API.

Workspaces.description: A kcp concept providing isolated Kubernetes-like control planes with their own API resources and objects, used as the organizational unit in Platform Mesh.

managed services.description: Services whose lifecycle is fully managed by a service provider through automated provisioning, scaling, and operations.

unmanaged services.description: Services not under automated lifecycle management, giving consumers direct control over deployment and operations.

digital twins.description: Virtual representations of physical or logical resources synchronized through the Platform Mesh API, enabling declarative management of real-world infrastructure.

marketplace.description: A catalog layer in Platform Mesh where service providers publish capabilities that service consumers can discover and bind to.

service runtime.description: The execution environment where a service provider deploys and operates managed capabilities for consumers.

servicelet.description: A lightweight automation component deployed alongside a service runtime that manages the lifecycle of capabilities on behalf of a service provider.

service coordinator.description: A control component that coordinates service lifecycle operations across multiple service runtimes within a service provider's account.

service.description: A capability or set of capabilities offered through the Platform Mesh API.

Managed Service Provider.description: A service provider that uses the Platform Mesh MSP pattern to manage multiple service runtimes and automate the full capability lifecycle for consumers.
